chapter_summary: Judas secretly agrees to hand Jesus over to his enemies. Jesus shares a special last meal with His disciples — the Passover — and gives them bread and a cup to remember Him by. The disciples argue about who is greatest, but Jesus teaches them that true greatness means serving others. Jesus warns Peter that he will deny Him. In the Garden of Gethsemane, Jesus prays with great anguish — 'Not my will, but thine, be done' — and an angel comes to strengthen Him. Judas arrives with a crowd and betrays Jesus with a kiss. Peter denies Jesus three times and weeps bitterly. Jesus is mocked, beaten, and brought before the Jewish council, who decide He must die.
main_lesson: Jesus chose to love us even when it was the hardest thing He ever did. Even when friends failed Him, He never stopped loving them — or us.

memory_verse.kjv: Not my will, but thine, be done.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus prayed: 'Father, if You are willing, take this cup away from Me. But I want what You want, not what I want.'

parent_guide: Luke 22 is one of the most emotionally layered chapters in all of Scripture — holding together tenderness and betrayal, institution and anguish, failure and grace. Key moments: the Last Supper (vv14-23) is a great opportunity to connect the Passover lamb to Jesus as our Passover sacrifice; the disciples' argument about greatness (vv24-30) shows how slowly even close followers grasp Jesus' upside-down kingdom; the Gethsemane prayer (vv39-46) is a window into Jesus' full humanity — He felt the weight of what was coming and chose obedience anyway. Peter's denial and weeping (vv54-62) is a powerful entry point for conversations about failure, shame, and the possibility of restoration (Peter was restored in John 21). The council scene (vv66-71) shows the religious leaders choosing to reject the very Son of God standing before them.
application_for_children: Jesus prayed 'not my will, but Yours' even when it was really, really hard. We can pray that same prayer whenever something is scary or difficult. And when we fail — like Peter did — Jesus still loves us and gives us another chance.

verses.kjv: Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.
verses.little_bible: It was almost time for the Passover feast — one of the most important celebrations of the Jewish year, when God's people remembered how He rescued them from Egypt long ago.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: It was almost time for the big Passover feast that God's people celebrated every year.
verses.meaning: The Passover feast celebrated God rescuing His people with a lamb's blood. Now Jesus — the true Lamb of God — was about to do something even greater.
verses.memory_phrase: God always has a rescue plan.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You for always making a way to rescue Your people.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever celebrated a special holiday that reminds you of something God did? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: The Passover reminded Israel of how God saved them with a lamb. How does Jesus fulfil that same picture?
verses.do_it_today: Look up the story of the first Passover in Exodus 12 and talk about what the lamb meant.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm Middle Eastern street at dusk, lanterns glowing, families carrying bread and herbs, children looking up at their parents with wonder. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
verses.little_bible: The chief priests and teachers of the law were plotting how to get rid of Jesus. They were afraid of Him — and afraid of the crowds who loved Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The religious leaders were making a secret plan to hurt Jesus because they were scared of Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus' enemies feared His influence. Their plotting was driven by jealousy and fear, not truth or justice.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew what was coming.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me trust You even when people around me try to do what is wrong.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the religious leaders were afraid of Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders feared Jesus because He was changing everything. Is there anything about Jesus that challenges the way we live?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for a leader or person in authority, that they would love truth and not be driven by fear.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures in dark robes whispering in a shadowed corridor, furtive expressions, candlelight flickering. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
verses.little_bible: Then something terrible happened — Satan entered the heart of Judas Iscariot, one of Jesus' twelve closest friends. Judas chose to let the enemy in.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Satan came into the heart of Judas, one of Jesus' twelve special friends.
verses.meaning: Satan can only enter when someone opens the door through selfishness, greed, or bitterness. This was a moment of tragic choice.
verses.memory_phrase: Guard your heart from the enemy.
verses.prayer: God, protect my heart. Help me say no to lies and greed.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Judas let Satan into his heart? What had he been choosing?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible warns us to guard our hearts. What are some ways we can keep our hearts safe from wrong thinking?
verses.do_it_today: Read Proverbs 4:23. Talk about one thing your family can guard against in your home this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man with a troubled, shadowed face walking alone on a cobblestone street at night, a dark presence suggested behind him. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
verses.little_bible: Judas went to the chief priests and temple guards and talked with them about how he could hand Jesus over to them secretly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Judas went to talk to Jesus' enemies about how to give Jesus to them.
verses.meaning: Judas made a deliberate plan. Betrayal doesn't usually happen all at once — it starts with small choices that lead somewhere dark.
verses.memory_phrase: Small wrong choices lead to big ones.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me choose right, even in the small things.
verses.discussion_question: What small choices do you think Judas had been making before this big wrong choice?
verses.family_discussion: How do we protect ourselves from drifting away from Jesus a little at a time?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one small right choice you can make today that helps you stay close to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure in a cloak meeting with robed religious leaders in a dimly lit room, heads bent together, coins glinting on a table. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
verses.little_bible: The chief priests were delighted! They agreed to pay Judas money in exchange for handing Jesus over to them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders were very happy and said they would pay Judas money.
verses.meaning: Judas sold Jesus for money — a heartbreaking picture of choosing things over people, and things over truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Love people more than money.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me never trade what really matters for things that don't last.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think money made Judas want to do something so wrong?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible says the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil (1 Timothy 6:10). How can we keep money in its right place in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone today that has nothing to do with money or reward.
verses.illustration_prompt: A leather pouch of coins placed on a table by a cloaked figure, candlelight gleaming on the silver pieces. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
verses.little_bible: Judas agreed to the deal. From then on he watched for the right moment to hand Jesus over when no crowd was around.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Judas said yes and started looking for the right time to hand Jesus over secretly.
verses.meaning: Judas was calculating and patient in his betrayal. Sin often works quietly, waiting for the right moment.
verses.memory_phrase: Stay close to Jesus every day.
verses.prayer: Jesus, keep me near You so I never drift into wrong.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Judas wanted to do this when no crowd was around?
verses.family_discussion: We can drift away from Jesus quietly, without big dramatic moments. How do we stay intentionally close to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Set a reminder today to spend a few minutes with God — not just when it feels important, but every day.
verses.illustration_prompt: A hooded figure watching from a distance as Jesus teaches a crowd in sunlit courtyard, expression calculating. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed.
verses.little_bible: The day finally arrived — the very day when the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed. Everything was about to change.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The day came when the special Passover lamb had to be ready.
verses.meaning: The timing was no accident. Jesus, the true Passover Lamb, would be sacrificed on the very day the Passover lambs were killed.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is our Passover Lamb.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for being my Passover Lamb and taking my punishment.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus chose the Passover time to give His life for us?
verses.family_discussion: In Exodus, the lamb's blood on the doorpost saved Israel. How does Jesus' blood save us?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a lamb and write 'Jesus is my Lamb' on it as a reminder of what He did.
verses.illustration_prompt: A white lamb against golden morning light, the temple in the background, people moving purposefully through a courtyard. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
verses.little_bible: Jesus sent Peter and John on an important errand. 'Go and get the Passover meal ready for us,' He told them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus sent Peter and John to prepare the Passover meal for everyone.
verses.meaning: Jesus was actively preparing for this final meal with His disciples — He knew what was coming and wanted everything to be right.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus prepares good things for us.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be ready and willing when You give me something important to do.
verses.discussion_question: Peter and John were trusted with a big job. How do you feel when someone trusts you with something important?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave His disciples things to do as part of His plan. How does God use us in His plans today?
verses.do_it_today: Help prepare something in your home — a meal, a table, or a special spot — and think about how Jesus prepares for us.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter and John walking with purpose through a sunny Jerusalem market street, baskets in hand, people and stalls around them. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
verses.little_bible: Peter and John asked, 'Where do You want us to get it ready, Lord?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter and John asked Jesus, 'Where do You want us to prepare?'
verses.meaning: A simple question of obedience — 'Where do You want us?' That is always a great question to ask God.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask God: 'Where do You want me?'
verses.prayer: Lord, I want to follow You. Show me where You want me today.
verses.discussion_question: What does it look like to ask God 'where do You want me?' in everyday life?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, how do you ask God about the decisions you make — big and small?
verses.do_it_today: Before you start something important today, pause and ask God to guide you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men standing before a gentle Jesus in a sun-dappled courtyard, looking at Him with attentive faces. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'When you go into the city, you will see a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house he enters.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told them to follow a man carrying a water jar into his house.
verses.meaning: Jesus gave specific, supernatural directions. He knew exactly what would happen — a reminder that He is in control of every detail.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows every detail.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I trust that You know all the details of my life too.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Peter and John felt when Jesus gave them such a specific sign?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew exactly what would happen before it did. How does knowing that help us trust Him with the details of our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one detail in your life that feels uncertain. Hand it over to Jesus in prayer today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man carrying a large clay water jar on his shoulder through a Jerusalem alleyway, two disciples following at a distance. Warm afternoon light. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'Say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher asks: where is the guest room where He can eat the Passover with His disciples?''
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told them to ask the house owner for a room to share the Passover meal.
verses.meaning: Jesus orchestrated this meal with care. He wanted to share this final Passover with those He loved most.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus loves to share special moments with us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You want to spend time with me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus wanted to celebrate the Passover with His friends before everything that was going to happen?
verses.family_discussion: Special meals together matter. What are some ways your family can make mealtimes feel meaningful?
verses.do_it_today: At your next family meal, take a moment for everyone to share one thing they are thankful for.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open door revealing a warm upper room with a long low table, lamplight glowing, a kind older man welcoming guests at the threshold. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'He will show you a large upper room, all set up and ready. Prepare the meal there.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man would show them a big upstairs room already set up. Make the meal ready there!
verses.meaning: God had already prepared the room. Sometimes God goes ahead of us and gets things ready before we even arrive.
verses.memory_phrase: God goes ahead to prepare the way.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for preparing things for me before I even know I need them.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever arrived somewhere and found that someone had already prepared something special for you? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: God prepared a room for Jesus. How do you see God preparing things for your family before you ask?
verses.do_it_today: Do something to prepare for someone else before they need it — a kind note, a tidy room, a meal ready.
verses.illustration_prompt: A spacious upper room with a long table, oil lamps burning, cushions arranged neatly, soft golden light through arched windows. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
verses.little_bible: Peter and John went and found everything exactly as Jesus had said. They got the Passover ready.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter and John went and found everything just as Jesus said. They prepared the meal.
verses.meaning: Jesus' word is always reliable. When He says something will happen, it does — exactly as He said.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' word always comes true.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me trust Your word — You are always right.
verses.discussion_question: Has God ever done something exactly like He said He would? How did it make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples found everything just as Jesus said. As a family, can you share an example of God being perfectly faithful to His word?
verses.do_it_today: Find one promise of God in the Bible today and read it aloud as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter and John in a sunlit market, then arriving at a house, expressions changing from focus to wonder as they find the room exactly as described. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
verses.little_bible: When the right time came, Jesus sat down at the table with His twelve apostles around Him. This would be their last meal together before everything changed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When it was time, Jesus sat down at the table with His twelve friends.
verses.meaning: Jesus gathered those He loved most for this final meal. He chose to be with people, not alone, even in His most important hour.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus loves to be with His friends.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I am glad You want to be with me. Thank You for never leaving me alone.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think it mattered to Jesus to have His friends with Him for this meal?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was about to go through the hardest thing imaginable, and He chose to spend the time before it with His people. How do we make sure we are truly present with the people we love?
verses.do_it_today: Put your phone away for dinner tonight and really be present with whoever is at your table.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus reclining at a long low table in a warm lamp-lit upper room, twelve disciples around Him, faces reflecting the golden light, a sense of closeness and holiness. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I have really, truly wanted to eat this Passover with you before I go through what is coming.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I really wanted to have this special meal with you before I suffer.'
verses.meaning: Jesus deeply longed for this moment with His disciples. He chose love and fellowship even on the eve of the worst suffering imaginable.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus deeply loves being with us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for wanting to be with me so much.
verses.discussion_question: How does it make you feel to know Jesus really, truly wanted to be with His friends that night?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus faced something terrifying, yet His heart was for His people first. How can we follow that example?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family that you love being with them — and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning forward at the table, eyes full of warmth and love, speaking to His disciples gathered close, lamplight catching the joy and tenderness on His face. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'I tell you, I will not eat this Passover again until everything it means is completely fulfilled in God's kingdom.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He would not eat this meal again until God's kingdom was fully here.
verses.meaning: Jesus was pointing to a future feast in God's kingdom — the great banquet when everything is made new. This meal was a beginning, not just an end.
verses.memory_phrase: The best feast is still coming.
verses.prayer: Lord, I look forward to the day when all things are made new and we celebrate together.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the feast in God's kingdom will be like?
verses.family_discussion: The Last Supper pointed forward to the Marriage Supper of the Lamb (Revelation 19). How does knowing a great future awaits us change how we live now?
verses.do_it_today: Read Revelation 19:9 together as a family — the promise of the great feast to come.
verses.illustration_prompt: A vision of a joyful banquet table stretching into bright heavenly light, people of all kinds gathered, Jesus at the head, a sense of completion and joy. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves:
verses.little_bible: Jesus took the cup, gave thanks to God, and said: 'Take this and share it among yourselves.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus took the cup, thanked God for it, and shared it with His disciples.
verses.meaning: Even here, Jesus paused to give thanks. Gratitude is a hallmark of Jesus' life — He always thanked His Father.
verses.memory_phrase: Always give thanks.
verses.prayer: Father, help me always be grateful, just like Jesus was.
verses.discussion_question: Why did Jesus give thanks before sharing the cup?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave thanks even before going to the cross. How can we cultivate gratitude in our family, especially in hard times?
verses.do_it_today: Before each meal today, pause and genuinely give thanks — not as a habit, but with a grateful heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding a simple clay cup, eyes looking upward in thankful prayer, disciples watching with reverent attention, warm lamplight. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I will not drink wine again until God's kingdom has come.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He would not drink from the vine again until God's kingdom came.
verses.meaning: Jesus was fasting ahead of His sacrifice and looking forward to the kingdom's fulfilment — every element of the Last Supper was filled with meaning and anticipation.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom is coming.
verses.prayer: Come, Lord Jesus! Let Your kingdom come.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that Jesus was looking forward to God's kingdom?
verses.family_discussion: We pray 'Your kingdom come' in the Lord's Prayer. What would it look like for God's kingdom to come in our home right now?
verses.do_it_today: Pray the Lord's Prayer slowly together and think about what each line really means.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking upward with quiet, steady hope, the glow of lamplight casting a warm halo around Him, a sense of both the present moment and the future. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus took the bread, gave thanks, broke it, and gave it to them saying: 'This is my body, which is given for you. Do this to remember Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus broke bread and said, 'This is my body, given for you. Remember Me when you do this.'
verses.meaning: Jesus gave His body for us — He chose to be broken so that we could be whole. This is the heart of everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' body was broken for me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for giving Your body for me. I will always remember You.
verses.discussion_question: When your church shares communion (the Lord's Supper), what do you think about?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said 'do this in remembrance of Me.' How can we keep the memory of what Jesus did fresh and real in our everyday life?
verses.do_it_today: The next time you eat bread, take a moment to think about Jesus giving Himself for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus' hands breaking a round loaf of bread, pieces offered to waiting hands around the table, golden lamplight, a moment of sacred stillness. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
verses.little_bible: In the same way, after supper Jesus took the cup and said: 'This cup is the new covenant sealed in my blood, which is poured out for you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus held the cup and said, 'This is the new promise sealed by my blood poured out for you.'
verses.meaning: A new covenant — a new promise — between God and people, sealed not with an animal's blood but with Jesus' own. This changes everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus made a new promise with His blood.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for the new covenant — for choosing me and making a way for me to belong to God.
verses.discussion_question: What is a covenant? How is God's new covenant through Jesus different from the old one?
verses.family_discussion: The old covenant required repeated sacrifices. The new covenant is once and for all through Jesus. How does that make us feel about what Jesus did?
verses.do_it_today: Read Jeremiah 31:31-34 together — God's promise of a new covenant, given hundreds of years before Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus lifting a simple clay cup, light catching it, disciples watching in reverent wonder, the atmosphere charged with the weight of something holy and new. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said something that must have felt like ice water: 'But look — the hand of the one who will betray Me is right here on this table with Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said sadly, 'One of you sharing this meal with Me is going to betray Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus knew exactly who would betray Him — and that person was right there, sharing His meal. Jesus loved him anyway and gave him every chance.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew, and still He loved.
verses.prayer: Jesus, even when I disappoint You, You still love me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Judas felt hearing Jesus say this?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew Judas would betray Him and still treated him as a friend. What does that tell us about how Jesus loves us, even when we fail?
verses.do_it_today: Is there someone who has been unkind to you? Ask Jesus to help you love them the way He loved Judas.
verses.illustration_prompt: A long supper table, Jesus speaking calmly, disciples looking at each other with worried faces, hands on the table, a sense of tension and sorrow. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'The Son of Man will go as God has planned. But how terrible it will be for the person who betrays Him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said this was all part of God's plan, but how sad for the one who would betray Him.
verses.meaning: God's plan cannot be stopped — even betrayal serves His greater purpose. But those who choose evil still bear responsibility for their choices.
verses.memory_phrase: God's plan always goes forward.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust that Your plans are bigger than anything that might go wrong.
verses.discussion_question: How can God's plan be moving forward even when people are doing wrong things?
verses.family_discussion: Judas made a real choice — he was not just a puppet. How do we hold together 'God is in control' and 'our choices matter'?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one way you have seen God bring good out of a hard situation.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a face of deep sorrow and quiet authority at the supper table, disciples frozen in attention around Him. Candlelight, solemn atmosphere. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they began to enquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.
verses.little_bible: The disciples started asking each other quietly: 'Is it me? Could it be you? Which one of us would do such a thing?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples started asking each other, 'Which one of us would do that?'
verses.meaning: Every disciple wondered if it could be them. That kind of honest self-examination is actually healthy — none of us is beyond temptation.
verses.memory_phrase: Check your own heart first.
verses.prayer: Lord, show me anything in my heart that is not right, so I can give it to You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it good to ask ourselves 'could I have done that wrong thing?'
verses.family_discussion: The disciples asked themselves a hard question. How can we as a family practise honest self-reflection without shame?
verses.do_it_today: Spend two minutes quietly asking God: 'Is there anything in my heart You want to change?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples at the supper table leaning toward each other, expressions of concern and searching, whispering, each glancing around in worry. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And there was also a strife among them, which of them should be accounted the greatest.
verses.little_bible: Then — incredibly — the disciples started arguing right then about which of them was the greatest!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Even then, the disciples started arguing about who was the most important.
verses.meaning: Even at the Last Supper, their pride surfaced. We are more like the disciples than we like to think.
verses.memory_phrase: Pride can sneak up on us anywhere.
verses.prayer: God, take away my pride and fill me with love for others instead.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the disciples argued about this at such an important moment?
verses.family_discussion: Pride can show up even in sacred moments. When have you seen pride show up unexpectedly in your own life?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that puts someone else first with no expectation of credit.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples at the supper table, some leaning forward with animated faces in argument, Jesus watching with patient, loving sadness. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them: 'The kings of the nations love to control people and make others call them 'great benefactors' and 'important leaders.''
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said that worldly kings like to be in charge and be called great.
verses.meaning: Jesus was contrasting two completely different models of leadership — power from the top down versus service from the bottom up.
verses.memory_phrase: God's greatness looks different.
verses.prayer: Jesus, show me what true greatness looks like in Your eyes.
verses.discussion_question: What does the world say a great person looks like? How is that different from what Jesus says?
verses.family_discussion: Our culture often celebrates power, fame, and control. What would it look like for our family to celebrate Jesus' kind of greatness?
verses.do_it_today: Look up one person in history or the Bible who was great by serving others. Share their story.
verses.illustration_prompt: A golden-robed king on a throne, surrounded by bowing people, contrasted in the next frame with Jesus washing feet. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, let him be as he that doth serve.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But you must not be like that. The greatest among you should act like the youngest, and the leader should act like the one who serves.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said the greatest person is the one who serves others.
verses.meaning: Jesus flips the world's idea of greatness completely upside down. The greatest in His kingdom are the most willing servants.
verses.memory_phrase: The greatest serve the most.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be great in Your way — by loving and serving others.
verses.discussion_question: Who is the greatest servant you know? What makes them great?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus defines greatness as service. How can each person in your family serve the others this week?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one act of service to do for someone in your home today, especially something no one will notice.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child cheerfully washing dishes, an older sibling setting the table, a parent kneeling to help tie shoes — small acts of service in a warm home. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked: 'Who is normally thought to be more important — the one eating at the table, or the one serving? It is the one eating, right? But I am here among you as the one who serves.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He was among them as a servant, not as the most important person.
verses.meaning: The Creator of the universe chose the role of servant. If Jesus served, how much more should we?
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to serve, not to be served.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You washed feet and served others. Help me do the same.
verses.discussion_question: How did Jesus show us by His life what serving really looks like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus, who deserved every honour, chose to serve. What does that inspire you to do?
verses.do_it_today: Read John 13:1-17 together as a family — the story of Jesus washing His disciples' feet.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling with a basin and towel, washing a disciple's dusty feet, the disciple's face full of wonder and humility. Warm lamplight. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said warmly: 'You are the ones who have stayed with Me through all my trials and hard times.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told His disciples: 'You have stayed with Me through all the hard times.'
verses.meaning: Jesus honoured their faithfulness. Sticking with Jesus through difficulty is one of the greatest things a disciple can do.
verses.memory_phrase: Stay with Jesus through the hard times.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me stay close to You even when life is difficult.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever stuck with someone through a hard time? What did that mean to them?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus noticed and treasured the disciples' loyalty. How does your family show loyalty to each other and to God in difficult seasons?
verses.do_it_today: Encourage someone today who is going through a hard time. Let them know you are staying with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small group of disciples around Jesus in varying scenes of difficulty — a stormy sea, crowds pressing in — always together. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus made an incredible promise: 'I am giving you a kingdom, just as my Father has given one to Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus promised His disciples they would share in His kingdom.
verses.meaning: The disciples who stayed with Jesus through hard times were given a kingdom. Faithfulness in the small things leads to extraordinary rewards.
verses.memory_phrase: Stay faithful — a kingdom is coming.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be faithful so that one day I can share in Your kingdom.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means to be given a kingdom by Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus rewards faithfulness with abundance. How does knowing there is a future reward change how we live for Jesus today?
verses.do_it_today: Read Matthew 25:21 together — the reward for faithful servants.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing broadly and joyfully to disciples at the supper table, a sense of extraordinary gift being given, faces lit with wonder. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.little_bible: Jesus promised: 'You will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones ruling alongside Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus promised they would eat at His table in His kingdom and sit on thrones with Him.
verses.meaning: These ordinary fishermen, tax collectors, and workers would one day share Jesus' throne. God loves to give glory to the humble.
verses.memory_phrase: God raises the humble to great things.
verses.prayer: God, I trust You to take care of my future. Help me be faithful now.
verses.discussion_question: Twelve ordinary people — thrones and a kingdom! What does that tell us about what Jesus thinks of us?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples seemed so ordinary. How does God use ordinary, faithful people for extraordinary things?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one ordinary thing you do — and ask God how He might use it for His kingdom.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glorious heavenly banquet, twelve figures seated on thrones of light, Jesus at the centre, a feast spread before them, a sense of joy and fulfilment. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat:
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'Simon, Simon — listen carefully. Satan has asked to test you like a farmer sifting wheat to shake you apart.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned Peter: 'Satan wants to shake you and test you very hard, Peter.'
verses.meaning: Jesus used Peter's full name — twice — to get his full attention. He was warning him about a very real, very fierce spiritual attack coming.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus warns us because He loves us.
verses.prayer: Lord, warn me when danger is near. I want to listen to You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus used Peter's name twice?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus warned Peter because He cared about him. How do we respond when God or a loved one warns us about something?
verses.do_it_today: Think about a warning God has put on your heart lately. Take it seriously today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning forward and speaking directly to a wide-eyed Peter at the supper table, a sense of urgency and deep love in His expression. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'But I have prayed for you, Peter, that your faith will not completely fail. And when you have turned back to Me, strengthen your brothers.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I have prayed for you, Peter. When you come back to Me, help the others.'
verses.meaning: Jesus did not pray that Peter would not fall — He prayed that Peter's faith would not finally fail. And He planned for Peter's restoration before the fall even happened.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus prays for you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for praying for me. Help my faith not to fail.
verses.discussion_question: How does knowing Jesus prays for you make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus planned for Peter's restoration before Peter even failed. What does that tell us about how Jesus deals with our mistakes?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person in your family by name — for their faith to stay strong this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with hand raised in gentle blessing over Peter, eyes full of knowing love, Peter looking moved and serious. Warm lamplight. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
verses.little_bible: Peter said with great confidence: 'Lord, I am ready to go with You even to prison and death!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said boldly, 'Lord, I will go with You even to prison or death!'
verses.meaning: Peter meant every word — and yet he did not know himself as well as he thought. Confidence without humility can crumble under pressure.
verses.memory_phrase: Know yourself — and trust God more than yourself.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be humble about my own strength and rely on Yours.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Peter was so sure he would never let Jesus down?
verses.family_discussion: Peter's confidence came from his heart — but not from his strength. Where should our confidence really come from?
verses.do_it_today: Philippians 4:13 says we can do all things through Christ who strengthens us. Memorise it together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter standing at the supper table, chest out, hand on his heart, speaking with passionate certainty, Jesus watching him with gentle, knowing love. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told him gently but honestly: 'Peter, I tell you — before the rooster crows today, you will deny three times that you even know Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told Peter: 'Before the rooster crows today, you will say three times that you don't know Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus knew what Peter would do before Peter did it — and He loved him anyway. This prediction was not a punishment; it was a warning wrapped in grace.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows us better than we know ourselves.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not to be overconfident. I need You every moment.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Peter felt when Jesus said this?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew Peter's failure before it happened and loved him through it. How does that shape how we should deal with our own failures?
verses.do_it_today: Read Psalm 103:13-14 together: God knows we are dust — and He is still compassionate.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking quietly to Peter, whose face falls from confident to troubled; a rooster faintly visible through an arched window in the background. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked His disciples: 'When I sent you out with no money, no bag, no extra sandals — did you lack anything?' They said, 'Not a thing!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked, 'When I sent you out with nothing, did you need anything?' They said, 'Nothing!'
verses.meaning: Jesus reminded them of how God had provided before — building their confidence in God's faithfulness before a very different season was about to start.
verses.memory_phrase: God has always provided before.
verses.prayer: God, remind me of all the times You have taken care of me.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time God provided exactly what you needed?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples looked back at God's faithfulness to build courage for the future. What is a story of God's faithfulness your family can look back on?
verses.do_it_today: Write down three times God has provided for your family and put the list somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small group of disciples on a sunny road, no bags, no extra clothes, faces full of peace and purpose. Birds in the sky, wildflowers along the path. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'But now things are different. If you have money or a bag, take them. And if you don't have a sword, sell your coat to buy one.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned them that things were about to get harder and they should be prepared.
verses.meaning: Jesus was warning that a new, harder season was beginning. He was not endorsing violence — He was telling them to be ready for hostility and opposition.
verses.memory_phrase: Be ready for what is coming.
verses.prayer: Lord, prepare me for hard times, and help me trust You through them.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do to prepare for something you know will be difficult?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the easy season was ending. As followers of Jesus, how do we prepare spiritually for difficult times?
verses.do_it_today: Read Ephesians 6:10-18 together — the armour of God, which is how we prepare as Christians.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples looking serious and attentive, Jesus speaking with quiet urgency; some reaching for bags and cloaks, the atmosphere shifting from warm celebration to readiness. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'This Scripture must be fulfilled in Me: 'He was numbered with the criminals.' Everything written about Me is reaching its fulfilment.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said that the old Scriptures about being treated like a criminal were coming true in Him.
verses.meaning: Isaiah 53:12 — written centuries before — was about to be fulfilled exactly. Nothing about Jesus' story was an accident; all of it was planned by God.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word always comes true.
verses.prayer: God, I trust Your Word. Help me believe that what You have promised will happen.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that something was written about Jesus hundreds of years before He was born?
verses.family_discussion: God planned Jesus' story long before it happened. How does that help us trust the parts of our own story we don't understand yet?
verses.do_it_today: Read Isaiah 53 together — the prophecy about the suffering servant written 700 years before Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: An ancient scroll with glowing text, and over it an image of Jesus — the words and the person coming together in one moment. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.
verses.little_bible: The disciples said, 'Lord, look — here are two swords!' Jesus replied simply, 'That is enough.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples showed Jesus two swords. He said, 'That is enough.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was not building an army. His 'enough' may have meant 'enough of this conversation' — His kingdom would not advance by the sword.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom does not advance by force.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust Your ways, which are not like the world's ways.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus stopped the conversation about swords?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus later told Peter to put his sword away. How do we deal with our problems God's way rather than by force or anger?
verses.do_it_today: Read Zechariah 4:6: 'Not by might nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the Lord.' Talk about what that means.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples holding up short swords, looking earnest; Jesus gently raising a hand to stop them, a quiet authority in His expression. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus left the upper room and went, as was His habit, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples followed Him into the night.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to the Mount of Olives to pray, as He always did, and His disciples came with Him.
verses.meaning: The Mount of Olives was Jesus' place of prayer. He went there habitually — and He went there now, on the hardest night of His life.
verses.memory_phrase: Make prayer your habit.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me pray not just in emergencies but every day as a habit.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think it mattered that Jesus went to pray at His usual place?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus had a habit of prayer. What prayer habits does your family have, and how can you strengthen them?
verses.do_it_today: Choose a regular time and place to pray this week — and stick to it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and disciples walking through a moonlit olive grove at night, the city of Jerusalem glowing in the distance behind them, dark trees overhead. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.
verses.little_bible: When they arrived, Jesus told them: 'Pray that you will not give in to temptation.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus told His disciples: 'Pray so you won't give in to temptation.'
verses.meaning: Prayer is our strongest defence against temptation. Jesus gave this instruction because He knew the night would test them all severely.
verses.memory_phrase: Prayer protects us from temptation.
verses.prayer: Father, help me pray every day so I am ready when temptation comes.
verses.discussion_question: Why does prayer help us when we are tempted to do something wrong?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told them to pray before the battle, not during it. How can our family prepare through prayer for the temptations we know we face?
verses.do_it_today: Identify one temptation you regularly face and pray about it before you are in the middle of it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing at the edge of a moonlit clearing in an olive grove, hand raised, speaking with quiet urgency to the disciples who are gathered around Him. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone's cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
verses.little_bible: Jesus walked a short distance away — about as far as you could throw a stone — and He knelt down and prayed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went a little further into the garden and knelt down to pray.
verses.meaning: Jesus needed to be alone with His Father. Even the most sacred moments of prayer often require stepping away from the crowd.
verses.memory_phrase: Find a quiet place to pray.
verses.prayer: Father, help me find a quiet place to be alone with You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus went a little further away to pray?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus sought solitude in prayer at His hardest moment. How does your family create space for quiet, private prayer?
verses.do_it_today: Find a quiet spot today — even just a corner of a room — and spend five minutes alone with God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling alone under a great olive tree in moonlit darkness, head bowed, a posture of complete surrender and anguish. Soft heavenly light touches His face. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.
verses.little_bible: He prayed: 'Father, if it is Your will, please take this cup of suffering away from Me. But — I want what You want, not what I want.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus prayed: 'Father, if You are willing, take this away. But not my will — Yours be done.'
verses.meaning: This is the greatest prayer ever prayed. Jesus felt the full weight of what was coming — and He chose the Father's will anyway. This is what perfect love and perfect obedience look like together.
verses.memory_phrase: Not my will, but Yours be done.
verses.prayer: Father, help me pray the same prayer Jesus prayed: not my will, but Yours be done.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think made it so hard for Jesus to say 'Your will be done'?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus had a will of His own — He genuinely did not want to go through what was coming. Yet He surrendered to His Father. When is it hardest for your family to pray 'Your will, not ours'?
verses.do_it_today: Pray 'not my will, but Yours be done' about one specific situation in your life right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling in anguished prayer under an olive tree, face lifted toward heaven, moonlight and a shaft of soft divine light breaking through the branches above Him, tears on His cheeks. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
verses.little_bible: Then, in that darkest moment, an angel from heaven appeared to Jesus and gave Him strength.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: An angel came from heaven to make Jesus stronger in His hard moment.
verses.meaning: God did not take the suffering away — but He sent strength for it. This is often how God answers our hardest prayers: not removal, but sustaining grace.
verses.memory_phrase: God sends help in our hardest moments.
verses.prayer: Lord, when things are really hard, please send Your help and strength to me too.
verses.discussion_question: God did not remove the hard thing from Jesus — He sent an angel to strengthen Him. How does that change how we pray about hard things?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God says 'I will help you through this' rather than 'I will take this away.' Can you share a time when God gave you strength instead of removal?
verses.do_it_today: Read Psalm 46:1: 'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.' Write it on something and put it where you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing angel kneeling beside Jesus in the dark garden, hands gently extended in comfort, Jesus' face beginning to show calm and strength amid the anguish. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
verses.little_bible: Jesus was in such deep anguish that He prayed even more intensely. And He was sweating so heavily it was like great drops of blood dripping to the ground.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was in so much pain from praying hard that He sweated like drops of blood falling to the ground.
verses.meaning: This was not performance — this was the full weight of bearing the sins of the world pressing on Jesus. He felt it completely. His love for us cost Him everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus felt real pain for us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I can never fully understand what You went through for me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus felt real pain and real agony in the garden. How does knowing that make you feel about how much He loves you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not face the cross with ease — He agonised over it. What does His choice to go through with it anyway tell us about the depth of God's love?
verses.do_it_today: Spend a quiet moment today simply thanking Jesus for what He chose to go through for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus prostrate on the dark ground of the garden, hands pressed into the earth, intense anguish and drops glistening on His brow, the moonlit garden silent around Him. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
verses.little_bible: When Jesus finished praying and came back to His disciples, He found them asleep — worn out from sorrow.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came back from praying and found His disciples had fallen asleep because they were so sad.
verses.meaning: The disciples had been overwhelmed with grief. Grief can make us fall asleep when we should be alert. Jesus understood, even in His own suffering.
verses.memory_phrase: Grief is real — but stay awake with Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I am sad and tired, help me stay close to You instead of shutting down.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples fell asleep from sadness. Have you ever been so sad or tired you just wanted to close your eyes?
verses.family_discussion: Grief can be exhausting and disorienting. How does your family support each other when someone is overwhelmed with sadness?
verses.do_it_today: Check on someone you know who might be going through grief or a really hard time today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Three disciples huddled together among the tree roots, fast asleep, cloaks wrapped around them; Jesus standing nearby looking at them with tired compassion. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
verses.little_bible: Jesus woke them gently: 'Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray, so that you will not fall into temptation!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus woke them and said, 'Get up and pray so you won't give in to temptation.'
verses.meaning: Even in the middle of His own agony, Jesus was thinking about them and their spiritual danger. That is the heart of a true shepherd.
verses.memory_phrase: Get up and pray — especially in hard times.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I am tired and sad, help me get up and pray instead of giving up.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think prayer is especially important when we are sad or afraid?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave the same instruction twice in this garden: 'Pray so you don't fall into temptation.' What does He know about prayer and temptation that we need to understand?
verses.do_it_today: If you feel too tired or sad to pray, try writing a prayer instead — sometimes that is easier.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gently touching the shoulder of a sleepy disciple, His own face still marked by prayer and anguish, moonlight through olive branches. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
verses.little_bible: While Jesus was still speaking, a mob arrived — and right at the front was Judas, one of the twelve, walking straight toward Jesus to kiss Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: While Jesus was still talking, a crowd arrived. Judas came forward to kiss Jesus.
verses.meaning: A kiss — normally the warmest of greetings — became the darkest betrayal. What should have shown love was used to mark Jesus for arrest.
verses.memory_phrase: True love never betrays.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be a person whose love is always true and never pretend.
verses.discussion_question: Why did Judas use a kiss — a sign of friendship — to betray Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Judas used the sign of friendship to do something terrible. How do we protect ourselves from being people who say one thing and do another?
verses.do_it_today: Think about whether your words match your actions today. Ask God to help you be consistent.
verses.illustration_prompt: A torchlit garden at night, a crowd with weapons in the background, Judas stepping forward toward Jesus, their faces close, a deeply sorrowful and conflicted expression on Judas. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at him and said: 'Judas — are you really handing over the Son of Man with a kiss?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus looked at Judas and said, 'Are you betraying me with a kiss?'
verses.meaning: Jesus called out the horror of using a sign of love for betrayal. But He spoke Judas' name — still with the dignity of one who is known and loved.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees through pretend love.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help my love for You and for others be real, not just words.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus called it out — that Judas used a kiss to betray Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus confronted the hypocrisy, but He did not rage. How do we respond with truth and dignity when someone betrays us?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person who has hurt or betrayed you. Ask God to work in their heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and Judas face to face in the torchlit night, Jesus calm and sorrowful, eyes full of truth, Judas' expression cracking under the weight of what he has done. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
verses.little_bible: The disciples saw what was happening and asked: 'Lord, should we fight? Should we use our swords?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples saw the crowd and asked Jesus, 'Should we fight with our swords?'
verses.meaning: The disciples' first instinct was to fight. But Jesus' kingdom does not advance by the sword. There is always a different way.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way is not always the fighting way.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me respond to conflict Your way, not with anger or force.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think about the disciples wanting to fight to protect Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: When someone threatens us or our family, the instinct is to fight back. How do we figure out what a Jesus-shaped response looks like?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a conflict in your life. Pray about how Jesus would want you to respond.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples clustered around Jesus in the torchlit garden, some with hands on sword hilts, looking to Jesus for direction, the armed crowd pressing in. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And one of them smote the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
verses.little_bible: Before Jesus could answer, one of the disciples swung his sword and cut off the ear of the high priest's servant.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: One disciple did not wait — he swung his sword and cut off a man's ear.
verses.meaning: Impulsive reactions — acting before thinking — often make things worse. Peter (we learn from John's Gospel it was Peter) struck before hearing Jesus' answer.
verses.memory_phrase: Wait for Jesus to lead before you act.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me wait and listen to You before I react.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when we act before we have heard from God or thought things through?
verses.family_discussion: Peter's impulsive act could have started a battle and got everyone killed. How do we teach ourselves to pause and pray before reacting?
verses.do_it_today: Before you respond to something difficult today, take three deep breaths and ask God what to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: A chaotic moment in the dark garden — a disciple's sword arm extended, a startled servant clutching his ear, torchlight and shouting figures all around. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Suffer ye thus far. And he touched his ear, and healed him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said immediately: 'Stop! No more of this.' And He reached out, touched the man's ear, and healed him — right there in the middle of His own arrest.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said 'Stop!' and then He touched the hurt man's ear and healed it right there.
verses.meaning: Even while being arrested, Jesus healed an enemy. This is the love of Jesus in action — unstoppable, uncontainable, even in His darkest hour.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus healed even in the moment of His own arrest.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your love never stops. Help me love others even when it costs me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus healed the servant's ear right then, even as He was being arrested?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus showed love to an enemy at the worst moment of His life. What is the most difficult moment of 'loving your enemy' your family has ever faced?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of kindness for someone who has been unkind to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus calmly reaching out to touch the ear of a wide-eyed, frightened servant amid the chaos of the arrest, a glow of healing light at His fingertips. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
verses.little_bible: Jesus spoke calmly to the chief priests, temple commanders, and elders who had come to arrest Him: 'Am I some kind of criminal, that you come with swords and clubs?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked the leaders calmly, 'Am I a thief, that you come against me with swords and clubs?'
verses.meaning: Jesus exposed the cowardice behind their massive show of force. He had been teaching in the temple every day — they never needed to come with weapons.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth does not need to hide.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me always stand calmly in truth, even when others come at me with force.
verses.discussion_question: Why did the leaders think they needed such a big, armed group to arrest one man?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus stood calmly before an armed mob. How do we stand calmly in truth when we feel outnumbered or intimidated?
verses.do_it_today: Read 2 Timothy 1:7: 'God did not give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and a sound mind.' Put it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing calm and upright before a circle of armed guards and robed leaders in the torchlit garden, His composure a sharp contrast to their nervousness. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'I was with you in the temple every day and you never touched Me. But this is your hour — and darkness has its moment now.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You never arrested me in the temple. But now it is the hour of darkness.'
verses.meaning: Darkness had its hour — but only an hour. Jesus permitted this moment because it was part of God's plan, not because evil had won.
verses.memory_phrase: Darkness has its hour, but God wins.
verses.prayer: God, even when it seems like darkness is winning, I trust You have a plan.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that this was 'the hour of darkness'?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes it looks like evil is winning. What does the Easter story tell us about who really wins in the end?
verses.do_it_today: Read John 16:33: 'In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking to the crowd with quiet authority, the night sky dark above the garden, torchlight casting dramatic shadows, a sense of something cosmic happening in the small scene. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then took they him, and led him, and brought him into the high priest's house. And Peter followed afar off.
verses.little_bible: Then they arrested Jesus, led Him away, and brought Him to the high priest's house. Peter followed at a distance — afraid to be too close.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They took Jesus away to the high priest's house. Peter followed far behind, afraid.
verses.meaning: Peter had said he would follow Jesus to death — now he followed from a distance. Fear has a way of closing the gap we thought we would never allow.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't follow Jesus from far away.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me stay close to You even when I am afraid.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Peter followed at a distance instead of staying close to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Fear made Peter keep his distance. What are the things that tend to make us keep our distance from God?
verses.do_it_today: Is there anything that is making you keep a distance from God? Talk about it — with God, and maybe with a trusted person.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus being led away by soldiers through a dark cobblestone street, and far behind, a hooded figure — Peter — trailing with his head down, the gap between them wide. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the hall, and were set down together, Peter sat down among them.
verses.little_bible: In the courtyard of the high priest's house, people had lit a fire and were sitting around it. Peter sat down right among them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People lit a fire in the courtyard and Peter sat down with them, trying to stay hidden.
verses.meaning: Peter crept in to stay warm — and to see what would happen to Jesus. He was trying to stay involved without being known, which is always a dangerous place to be.
verses.memory_phrase: Half-hidden is a dangerous place to be.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be brave enough to be fully known as Yours.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it hard to be openly 'one of Jesus' people' sometimes?
verses.family_discussion: Peter tried to follow without being seen. Are there moments when we are ashamed to be known as Christians? What can help us be bolder?
verses.do_it_today: Look for one opportunity today to let someone know you love Jesus — in a natural, genuine way.
verses.illustration_prompt: A courtyard at night, a glowing fire in the centre, people seated around it, Peter among them trying to look ordinary, his face half in shadow. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: But a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire, and earnestly looked upon him, and said, This man was also with him.
verses.little_bible: A servant girl spotted Peter sitting by the fire. She looked at him carefully and said, 'This man was with Jesus too!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A servant girl looked at Peter and said, 'He was with Jesus too!'
verses.meaning: Even in the flickering firelight, Peter was recognisable as a follower of Jesus. We are more visible than we think.
verses.memory_phrase: People notice who you belong to.
verses.prayer: Lord, let people see Jesus in me — and help me not to run from that.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the servant girl recognised Peter as one of Jesus' followers?
verses.family_discussion: Peter's connection to Jesus was visible even when he was trying to hide. What marks us out as followers of Jesus today?
verses.do_it_today: Think about what people see when they look at your life. Is your connection to Jesus visible?
verses.illustration_prompt: A servant girl leaning forward with firelight on her face, pointing at a shadowed Peter; other people around the fire turning to look. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
verses.little_bible: But Peter denied it: 'Woman, I don't know Him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said, 'Woman, I don't even know Him!'
verses.meaning: The first denial. Peter's fear overrode his love — but this was not the end of his story. Jesus had already prayed for him.
verses.memory_phrase: Fear can make us say what we don't really mean.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me never be ashamed of You. Give me courage to say I am Yours.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever said you didn't know something or someone because you were afraid? How did you feel afterwards?
verses.family_discussion: Peter denied Jesus out of fear. How do we help each other be brave enough to own our faith, even in difficult situations?
verses.do_it_today: Practise saying 'I am a Christian' out loud. Sometimes we just need to practise being bold.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter's face tense with fear in the firelight, hand raised as if to push the accusation away, the servant girl looking doubtful. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
verses.little_bible: A little while later, someone else looked at Peter and said, 'You are one of them too!' Peter said, 'Man, I am not!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A little while later someone else said, 'You are one of them!' Peter said, 'I am not!'
verses.meaning: The second denial. Each denial made the next one easier. That is how patterns of wrong behaviour work — each step away from truth makes the next step harder to avoid.
verses.memory_phrase: Each wrong step makes the next one easier.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me stop wrong patterns quickly before they grow.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Peter found it easier to deny Jesus the second time?
verses.family_discussion: Small compromises lead to bigger ones. How do we recognise and stop patterns of wrong behaviour before they become habits?
verses.do_it_today: Is there a small wrong pattern in your life? Ask someone you trust to help you stop it before it grows.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter by the fire, a different person pointing at him, Peter's posture defensive, pulling his cloak around him. The fire casts unsteady light. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this man also was with him: for he is a Galilaean.
verses.little_bible: About an hour later, another person said confidently: 'I am certain — this man was with Jesus. You can tell by the way he talks — he is from Galilee!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: An hour later someone said firmly, 'This man was definitely with Jesus — you can tell by his accent!'
verses.meaning: Even an accent betrayed Peter's connection to Jesus. We cannot fully hide who we are — our background, our speech, our manner all reveal us.
verses.memory_phrase: How you talk reveals who you are with.
verses.prayer: Lord, let the way I speak and act show that I belong to You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'sounding like a Galilean' means? What does your speech reveal about who you are?
verses.family_discussion: Our words reveal where we come from and who we spend time with. What do your family's words reveal about your relationship with God?
verses.do_it_today: Pay attention to your words today. Do they sound like someone who spends time with Jesus?
verses.illustration_prompt: A person at the fire jabbing a confident finger at Peter, others leaning in to listen; Peter's expression close to panic. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
verses.little_bible: Peter said, 'Man, I have no idea what you're talking about!' And at that very moment — while the words were still coming out of his mouth — a rooster crowed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said, 'I don't know what you mean!' And right then a rooster crowed.
verses.meaning: The moment Peter finished his third denial, the rooster crowed — exactly as Jesus had said. God's word is always precise.
verses.memory_phrase: What Jesus says will always come true.
verses.prayer: Lord, Your words are always true — help me trust them and live by them.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the rooster crowing at exactly that moment was so important?
verses.family_discussion: Every word Jesus spoke was fulfilled precisely. What does that tell us about the trustworthiness of Jesus' promises to us?
verses.do_it_today: Find one promise Jesus made and read it aloud, trusting that it is just as certain as His word about the rooster.
verses.illustration_prompt: The courtyard in pre-dawn light, a rooster on a rooftop crowing loudly, Peter frozen mid-sentence below, the sound cutting through the air. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
verses.little_bible: At that moment the Lord turned and looked directly at Peter. And Peter remembered — Jesus had said, 'Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus turned and looked right at Peter. Peter remembered what Jesus had said.
verses.meaning: The look of Jesus — not a look of anger, but of knowing love. Jesus did not say a word. He simply looked at Peter, and Peter was undone by that gaze.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus looks at us with love, even when we fail.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I fail, help me feel Your loving look more than my shame.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the look on Jesus' face was when He turned to Peter?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus looked at Peter with eyes that knew everything Peter had done — and Peter was not destroyed. He was broken in the best way. How does God's loving gaze heal us when we fail?
verses.do_it_today: If you are carrying shame about something, come before Jesus and let Him look at you with love. Pray and let His grace in.
verses.illustration_prompt: Across a courtyard, Jesus turning His head to look at Peter — their eyes meeting in the torchlight — Jesus' expression full of sorrow, knowledge, and unchanging love. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
verses.little_bible: Peter walked out and wept — he cried and cried with a deep, broken, bitter grief.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter went outside and cried and cried with deep sadness.
verses.meaning: This is one of the most moving moments in the New Testament. Peter's weeping was the beginning of his restoration — not the end of his story. Brokenness before God is always a beginning.
verses.memory_phrase: Tears of sorrow can be the start of healing.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I fail You and feel the weight of it, help me run to You, not away from You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Peter cried so hard? What was he feeling?
verses.family_discussion: Peter's bitter tears were not the end — he went on to become the leading apostle. How does God use our moments of deep failure and repentance as turning points?
verses.do_it_today: Read 2 Corinthians 7:10: 'Godly sorrow brings repentance that leads to salvation.' Pray for the kind of sorrow that leads to change.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter alone in a narrow alley in the early dawn, face in his hands, shoulders shaking, tears on his cheeks — a picture of real, broken grief and the beginning of restoration. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the men that held Jesus mocked him, and smote him.
verses.little_bible: The men who were holding Jesus began to mock Him and hit Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The men holding Jesus made fun of Him and hit Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus — the Son of God, Lord of all creation — was mocked and beaten by ordinary soldiers. He bore it. He did not call down armies of angels. He bore it for us.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus bore it all for us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I cannot imagine what You went through. Thank You for bearing it for me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus had power to stop all of it. Why did He choose to let it happen?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was mocked and hurt by people He came to save. How does that change the way you think about what God thinks of you?
verses.do_it_today: Spend a moment in quiet worship — just thanking Jesus for what He chose to go through.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing bound in a guard room, soldiers around Him mocking and jeering; Jesus' face calm and dignified even in the pain. Storybook watercolour style.
