verses.kjv: And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
verses.little_bible: They put a blindfold over His eyes, hit Him, and taunted Him: 'Prophesy! Which one of us just hit You?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They blindfolded Jesus, hit Him, and said mockingly, 'Tell us who just hit You!'
verses.meaning: Their cruel game was asking Jesus to use His power as a prophet — not knowing that He could have ended their game with a word. His restraint was a sign of supreme power, not weakness.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' silence was not weakness — it was love.
verses.prayer: Lord, when others are cruel to me, help me respond the way You did — with quiet dignity.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus could have answered them and proved everything. Why do you think He stayed silent?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes the most powerful response is silence and dignity. When has your family chosen to respond to unkindness with grace instead of fighting back?
verses.do_it_today: Read Isaiah 53:7: 'He was oppressed and afflicted, yet he did not open his mouth.' Think about what that kind of restraint looks like.
verses.illustration_prompt: Blindfolded Jesus standing in quiet, dignified endurance as rough hands reach toward Him; His posture upright and still amid the cruelty. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
verses.little_bible: They said many other terrible, disrespectful things against Jesus as well.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said many other unkind and disrespectful things to Jesus.
verses.meaning: The mockery went on. Jesus heard every word. And He kept choosing love. Every insult was paid for with His blood.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus heard it all and kept loving.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You endured every insult so I could be forgiven. I love You.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Jesus felt during all of this? What kept Him going?
verses.family_discussion: Hebrews 12:2 says Jesus endured the cross 'for the joy set before him.' What was the joy He was looking forward to?
verses.do_it_today: Read Hebrews 12:1-3 together. Let Jesus' example of endurance encourage you in whatever you are facing.
verses.illustration_prompt: A circle of jeering, gesturing soldiers around the bound Jesus, their faces twisted in mockery; Jesus standing still with a quiet, sorrowful dignity. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And as soon as it was day, the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
verses.little_bible: At daybreak, the leaders of the people — the elders, chief priests, and scribes — assembled and led Jesus into their council chamber.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When day came, the religious leaders brought Jesus before their council.
verses.meaning: After a night of mockery and beating, Jesus now faced a 'trial.' But this council had already decided the verdict before they asked a single question.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus faced unfair judgement so we could be truly forgiven.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You for going through an unfair trial so I could be found not guilty.
verses.discussion_question: Is it right to decide a verdict before hearing the evidence? Why not?
verses.family_discussion: The council was not really looking for truth — they were looking for permission to do what they had already decided. How do we make sure we seek truth rather than just confirming what we already think?
verses.do_it_today: Practice listening to understand before responding today — especially in a disagreement.
verses.illustration_prompt: A formal council chamber at dawn, robed elders and priests seated on raised benches, Jesus led in by guards, a single figure of calm truth in the midst of hostile power. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
verses.little_bible: They demanded: 'Tell us — are You the Messiah?' Jesus replied: 'If I tell you, you will not believe Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They asked, 'Are you the Messiah?' Jesus said, 'If I tell you, you won't believe me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus knew the truth would not change their minds — they had already chosen not to believe. Truth rejected does not become untruth.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the truth, whether people believe it or not.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me believe and receive Your truth, not push it away.
verses.discussion_question: Why would Jesus saying He was the Messiah not change their minds?
verses.family_discussion: People can reject truth even when it is standing right in front of them. How do we keep our own hearts open to truth that might challenge us?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something true that you have been avoiding? Ask God to help you face it with courage and trust.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing before the seated council, the leaders leaning forward with hostile faces, Jesus calm and clear, the contrast between human authority and divine truth vivid. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.
verses.little_bible: He continued: 'And if I ask you anything, you will not answer Me — and you will not let Me go.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'And if I ask you anything, you will not answer me or let me go.'
verses.meaning: Jesus revealed their hypocrisy — this was not a genuine hearing; it was a pre-determined outcome dressed up as law.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees through hypocrisy.
verses.prayer: God, keep my heart honest. I don't want to say I want truth but really avoid it.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have a 'trial' where the verdict is already decided?
verses.family_discussion: How do we make sure our family conversations are genuinely honest, not just saying what is expected?
verses.do_it_today: Have one honest conversation today — really listen to the other person rather than waiting to say what you already think.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking steadily at the council, His words measured and clear, the council members shifting uncomfortably. A lamp on the wall flickers. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said something extraordinary: 'From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the Almighty God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'From now on the Son of Man will sit at the right hand of God's power.'
verses.meaning: Even in His darkest moment, Jesus declared His ultimate destiny — not defeat, but exaltation. The cross was not the end. The throne was coming.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is seated in power at God's right hand.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I praise You — You are not defeated. You are enthroned.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the council felt when Jesus said He would be seated at God's right hand?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' 'lowest' moment was actually the beginning of His highest exaltation. How does the resurrection change how we see Jesus' suffering?
verses.do_it_today: Read Hebrews 1:3 together: 'After making purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A vision within the scene: above the council chamber, a glimpse of a heavenly throne, a glorious figure seated at the right hand of blinding light — the contrast with the humble prisoner below. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
verses.little_bible: They all asked together: 'So You are the Son of God?' Jesus answered: 'You yourselves are saying that I am.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They all asked, 'Are you the Son of God?' Jesus said, 'You are saying that I am.'
verses.meaning: Jesus confirmed what they were saying. 'You say it' was a solemn, clear affirmation. He was the Son of God — and He would not deny it even now.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus never denied who He was.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are truly the Son of God. I will not be ashamed to say so.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus confirmed He was the Son of God even when it would cost Him His life. What does that tell us about how certain He was?
verses.family_discussion: Peter had just denied knowing Jesus. Now Jesus confirmed exactly who He was before His accusers. What contrast does that make? How does Jesus' courage inspire you?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today that you believe Jesus is the Son of God — and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The entire council leaning forward, Jesus meeting their gaze without flinching, His simple words filling the chamber with weight. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And they said, What need we any further witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.
verses.little_bible: They said to one another: 'We don't need any more witnesses — we have heard it from His own lips!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders said, 'We heard it ourselves from His mouth — that is all we need!'
verses.meaning: They used His truthfulness against Him. Jesus' honest confession of who He was became their 'evidence.' But their verdict could not change who He really was — or what He was about to accomplish.
verses.memory_phrase: What seemed like defeat was really victory.
verses.prayer: Jesus, what they meant for evil You turned to good. Thank You for the cross.
verses.discussion_question: The leaders thought they had won. But who really won in the end?
verses.family_discussion: Genesis 50:20 says 'You meant evil against me, but God meant it for good.' How does that verse apply to the cross? How might it apply to your family's hard times?
verses.do_it_today: Read 1 Corinthians 1:18: 'The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.' Discuss what that means.
verses.illustration_prompt: The council members rising from their seats, animated and certain they have won; Jesus standing quietly in the centre, the hint of something eternal and undefeated in His expression. Storybook watercolour style.
